description_long: This object is a heavy, multi-barreled machine gun with a prominent rotary barrel cluster at the front, reminiscent of a Gatling or minigun design. The barrel assembly consists of three parallel cylindrical tubes, each reinforced with circular rings, suggesting a high rate of fire and robust construction. The main body is bulky and angular, with two large cylindrical components mounted on either side, likely representing ammunition or power housings. At the rear, a rectangular frame and a ribbed cylindrical section may serve as a mounting or handling point. The front end features a distinctive angular prong or blade, possibly for breaching or as a bayonet-style attachment. The entire object is finished in a matte black color, giving it a tactical and utilitarian appearance suitable for urban combat scenarios.
description: A heavy, multi-barreled rotary machine gun with a matte black finish, featuring a prominent barrel cluster and angular front prong. The design is robust and tactical, with large side housings and a rectangular rear frame.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular arrangement of the three barrels and the symmetrical side housings, with the angular prong partially visible at the top.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the length of the barrel cluster, the large side housing, and the sharply angled front prong.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the ribbed cylindrical rear section, the rectangular frame, and the symmetry of the side housings.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the full length of the barrels, the side housing, and the angular prong at the front.
attribute_barrel_type: rotary multi-barrel cluster with reinforcing rings
